channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /smg/ - stock market genera — 399 replies — 1596.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ - stock market genera

Open thread • Replies: 399 • Updated: 2026-07-03 20:07 UTC

OP

Believe edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>Previous:
>>62440882

Early replies (first 3)

62441894
OH MY FUCKING GOD
MY FIRST SHORT, ON MEMORY
IS THIS REAL

62441896
Various stocks have massive issues. K1, PFIC, or Collectable taxation. What a headache. On that note I want to double check, GDXU, GDX, SILJ, and SIL are safe right? I don't want a headache from these ETFs.

>>62441443
Uhh what, how???

>>62441714
Hmm a very curious situation, big groups have to choose between
>pump it so Michael Burry loses, but retail gets wealthy
>dump it so retail loses, but Michael Burry finally wins a new big short

62441898
did you guys have a good pride month?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442944
>>62442918
>anthropic
Didn't hear about the delay
>openAI
If that company was doing so poorly, wouldn't they want to IPO as soon as possible to gain exit liquidity?
>meta
That's pretty ambiguous, I don't think they've claimed to have cancelled order or anything, just that they'll start selling excess compute, which is a way to compete with other providers in new space and way to gain some liquidity/revenue stream, but I'm not sure anyone really knows what Meta's trying to do here. Unless they're scaling down their buildout, then it doesn't mean much
>MSFT
Not sure how that's related negatively to AI, it's just another normal layoff. If anything these tend to be people laid off and planned to be replace by AI.
>Apple
Tim Cook is getting squeezed for once and is mad. Not all too sure whether he'll even get his china chips. They'll just shift the price increase to customer that will eat it up
>>62442920
>why would you buy cheap thing
Why won't you just want and buy it when it becomes inevitably cheaper the next trading day? I don't get it. If the demand just got destroyed, shouldn't semis crash now?

62442953
>>62442944

you "direction brain" its an NPC disease that's really common

>line go up thing good
>line go down thing bad
>buy good thing expensive
>sell cheap thing bad

I'm not saying chips are going to 0 dude. I'm saying they were expensive/overpriced and you are supposed to buy them when they are cheap instead of bagholding as exit liquidity after you buy the exact top of parabolic hype bullshit

QCOM is what I'm most interested in buying. I had a nice stack of it Friday and it pumped too hard at close so I sold it. I'll rebuy it if it goes lower.

62442956
>>62442953

I keep saying Friday because I don't live in the real world and don't even know what day it is. I meant thursday but my brain thinks it was Friday since markets closed today

62442961
>>62442932
You're a little late

>>62442924
>chips are definitely going to pull back
the mother of all dumps coming monday
we kept it flat for americas birthday, but that will only make bobo coom 3x as hard.

62442964
>>62442837
They do give out infinite margin. Just buy the stocks that go up and don't buy the ones that go down. Boom. Margin go up. Just never be wrong. It's that simple.

62442969
>>62442961
Yeah I know I'm fairly late into it. Though if we really think about it, the oil consequences haven't really hit yet. And I'm thinking about holding this for a year or more.
Memory and chips just seems really fucking scary now so I wasn't sure where else I'd put my money in. I was in EU def for a long time but that is also a fucking pitfall.

62442971
>>62442953
>I'm not saying chips are going to 0 dude. I'm saying they were expensive/overpriced and you are supposed to buy them when they are cheap instead of bagholding as exit liquidity after you buy the exact top of parabolic hype bullshit
Agreed. That's why you buy, hold and then offload bags in couple of quarters.

Though if you think that demand has been clearly destroyed in last 2 weeks, shouldn't the market reflect that and keep dumping semis?

62442976
>>62442971

>shouldn't the market reflect it

it did? everything got fucking obliterated

>why would you buy QCOM at a low
because its under 20 p/e, like 3% div a solid value play and its narrative wasn't some jeet saying "line go infinity we moon apes strong"

62442978
>>62442971
the market is forward looking. the reason for the dumps is everyone's nervous and trying to be the first one out the door before the first less than perfect earnings print. it could be another 6 to 12 months before we get something like that though.

62442985
>>62442953
>buy the least expensive luxury good
You should know from your own personal life that this is how you get raped.

62442987
>>62442985

I actually sell luxury goods to faggots. I would never would buy them. I have like 400 purses in a pile in the floor right now and my phone just gives me alerts all day long of stupid goys spending money for them

62443008
Ironic stuff.

62443014
It's 87° in my house rn

62443017
>>62443008
Wonder if they'll put some sacrificial lamb in a cell and call it a day. The big fish didn't get jailed for fucking kids or crypto rugpulls and they won't sit for this either.

62443019
>>62443008

62443020
>>62443008
Kek, after distorting the market with their jewish treasury Scotty Bussy tricks, now they're going full commie chasing after refiners. Basically blaming refiner for the shit that retarded administration engineered. All to preemptively redirect the blame for what is about to happen.
Lmao.
They're panicking up there. And if they panic, you should too.

62443023
IM SO FUCKING BORED OPEN THE CASINO BACK UP

REEEEE!!!!!!!!!!

62443026
>>62443023
Aren't futures still open or is that only in the EU?

62443028
>>62443023
Go work out.

62443031
>>62442987
Well then you should know that while its all some sort of noncery, buying the cheapest luxury brand is the heaviest raping where its literally just the name and probably a bigger piece of shit than you could get from Alibaba.

If you're going to buy luxury which you should do rarely, it should be actual luxury.

In the same way, the cheapo chip stock is probably a literal piece of shit thats up solely because its a chip stock.

Its not a "value" play any more than a boring black Michael Coors bag made by Thai slaves for $3 is.
At least with LV or Gucci or something you might get a cool design and have it made by thai slaves that have been imported to italy.

62443032
>>62443026
If I can't say trade my life savings on 0dtes then it ain't worth it
>>62443028
Cope

62443033
Silver

62443038
there is not a single rain cloud in the sky and there's lightning strikes, some close. this is crazy i've never seen this

62443042
>>62443032
>0dtes
based

62443045
>>62443038
Cloudless lightning is very potent and long-range, don't be standing out in a filed or something that would make you a lightning rod. The record holder is 515 miles long and went across 3 states.

62443049
>>62443047
>>62443047
>>62443047

62443058
>>62443020

There’s definitely a whiff of panic in the air now. The refiners on the west coast are running at running very hot at about 98% utilisation. About 90% of the east coast. So the US isn’t just exporting its SPR reserves to support the world. It’s having to run refineries for gas, diesel and the rest with NO cushion for a hurricane or outage. The loss of not just gulf oil is being felt. It’s the gulf REFINING CAPACITY.

So it’s a double countdown. It seems like the market stain will become undeniable in 1-3 weeks. And if there’s any sort of refinery capacity outage (they happen that’s why they keep spare capacity). Then it will happen fast.

62443101
>>62443008
>>62443019

they told you oil prices would come down and you laughed at them and now you are surprised its all fake and gay. I don't know why you guys try to actively fight against something you know is fake and retarded. oil is going to be cheaper than it was before the war even with the strait closed while you guys blow up your gambling account and act confused.

they can make the price of paper oil fucking negative if they want lmao.

>>62443031
>cheapo stock pick is a shitcoin

I mean I gamble on companies that are like 500B+ market cap. none of them are going to pack up in the middle of the night and ghost like Enron (probably)

62443655
>>62442745
love all these retards who think bear market after several a red day when we bounce back right up

item.title: >if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT — 73 replies — 98.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-07-03 19:24 UTC

OP

>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Early replies (first 3)

62433628
But saaaaaar isnt this normal in india and all civilized places? Only in the so called united states of america they kick their kids out

62433637
>>62433628
anything that isnt jewish is indian because.... because it just is, okay!!!!

62433641
All you need is a half million dollar down payment and you can get your mortgage payment down to $4,250 per month on your loan against a million dollar house. Then you can live in the desert on top of your neighbors with trees that will fall over during the first rains and slight breeze because they don't belong in a desert.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62441264
>>62438159
I'm happy he didn't ruin their lives.

62441268
>>62436914
Everyone with this mentality is going to be removed from the gene pool. That is your bootstraps.

62441344
The weirdest thing about this board are mindbroken losers like defending boomers for the keks and lels.

62441345
>>62441264
>give like no one else
But not to your kids!

62441355
>>62441345
You can't read

62441358
>>62441344
Call your uncle back you little whiny brat.

62441547
>>62433637
I was referring to his grammar.

62441559
>>62433626
Boomers are entitled to this piece of land. There is nothing you can do about it.
You own nothing you happy.

62442042
>>62441268
These retards (a LARPer, obviously, but there are people who genuinely believe this shit) don't realize that their kids are competing with someone who's parents are professionals from Nigeria, Syria, or some other 3rd World shithole hell-bent on their kids having a better life than they had, who will do everything to ensure they start out on third base, while you're keeping your kid in the on-deck circle.

62442046
>>62442042
>professionals from Nigeria, Syria, or some other 3rd World shithole
And you can bet they're not going to spend their retirement (and their kids' inheritance) going on cruises or pursuing other boomer money-sinks, probably viewing themselves as a failure if they don't leave their kids the biggest inheritance they can.

62442134
>>62442046
Yes, Nigerians are well known globally for their fiscal responsibility and sense of duty.

62442194
in my early 20s my dad told me that he was not going to support me from here on out, and that i needed to become totally independent. he made it abundantly clear, i was totally on my own.

so i said okay, and i spent my 20s basically grinding in college with basically zero support from him. i took on student loans, pursued a stem degree, got internships, i spent dozens of hours a week studying- with no social life. after all, why would i party, date, or make friends in college when those would, objectively speaking, only distract from my coursework? which would, objectively speaking, run the risk of imperiling my career prospects and financial future? in other words, why would i take on frivolous social activities when i had no fallback plan in case i got bad grades or dropped out of stem? i determined a social life was not a risk worth taking, since there was no backup plan available to me.

so i had the most boring college experience possible and now 5 years after college, i make very decent money in a technical field. but instead of playing golf or pickleball or ultimate frisbee in boater shoes like my country club attending WASP-y peers do, i just emulate games from the 2000s (currently playing monster hunter dos) in my pajamas and spend all my spare income trading risky leveraged positions for extra money. because i am basically disillusioned and have no interest in spending any money in hobbies, and i just want to retire as soon as possible. because my life since college has been nothing but grinding, when i work i grind, when i daytrade i grind. so i want to retire as quickly as possible so i get to relax.

my dad has recently started to call me, almost frantic, aggressively asking me why i'm not pursuing a relationship, a wife, a family, and so on. and i really don't know how to explain he basically did everything in his power to create the situation he is not happy with.

62442314
>>62433626
I'm still paying m onthly child support for a 24 year old becaus ethey are in full time education. Just found out this week that they are now doing a masters. I had been looking forward to being emancipated from this, not so much the giving money to my child but having to send it to someone who cheated on me 20 years ago and make the divorce a 10 year long waste of life.

What do I think of this generation that feels they can live off their parents forever? Well I started workinng at 16 and living on my own at 17 (very menial work, cleaning nightclub bathrooms) but as a child I worked beside my father herding farm animals and feeding them every day. My child has never lifted a finger to help me with anything and has in their entire life only worked three months half shift in a shop and walked out of that job because they were asked to mop a floor.

So what are the consequences? Well I am leaving my child nothing in my will but to my fathers relatives. They won't be expecting that but I have never seen that child show the slightest respect for my families heritage or me.

What do I think of zoomers/milennials in general? People like OP? Guess. I don't give a fuck about them and no one should.

62442325
>>62441344
The real losers we met along the way was (you). Pathetic lazy gambling addicts who sat scratching their arses doing pointless degrees and farting on about 'making it'

62442338
>>62442194
You're asw much of a failure as a son as you are as a human being. Fairly typical for a generation to spend a decade on the internet denigrating their parents who wiped their arses and fed them and read them bedtime stories, birthday presents, christmas presents, books, toys, dental etc etc. .

I hope your father disinheirits you you self pitying bum, it is all you deserve. Why should he be loyal to you when you have zero loyalty to him? You're a sickening waste of oxygen.
>>62442194
>(currently playing monster hunter dos) in my pajamas and spend all my spare income trading risky leveraged positions for extra money

62442346
>>62435117
kys do something nice for your parents for once you fucking parasite, I can only assume they tolerate you because they know you are diagnosed mentally disabled

62442364
>>62441222
At a point where every dollar charged means they'll have to pay 2 back to some kike bank.
You can teach that without ruining their start. I started when my daughter was 6 and soon my son will follow: gamified everything, wooden money, savings, deposits, inflation, volume discounts, etc. In a few years we'll be using real markets. And we'll outkike the kikes if needs be, but they're starting their adulthood debt-free provided they grow up responsible. If they waste it then I may change my mind, I guess I'll have to supervise their finance a bit until they convince me they know how to play the game and understand money.

62442366
>>62442346
You first faggot, I’m sure he isn’t a pain to be around and actually might be a joy to his parents which is why they let him live there

62442380
>>62435510
Also all his kids and even his kids spouses work FOR HIM.

Rachel Cruze (daughter) - Co host on his show, has own show her husband Winston Cruze is the CEO of Ramsey Solutions, a financial education and counseling company

Daniel Ramsey - President of his father's company, Ramsey Solutions

Denise Whitmore (daughter) - Director of the Ramsey Family Foundation at Ramsey Solutions

And all three of them OWN Ramsey solutions which is where Dave sells his shit to his viewers

62442389
>>62442338
im not ungrateful to my father at all. you seem to be totally misunderstanding. i am perfectly content with the outcome. i simply reacted to the events in front of me in the ways that made sense with the information i had at the time.

what my post pointa out, i am unsure of how to articulate the idea to my father, who is not happy. "if you wanted a socialite son with WASP-y hobbies and a fiance cut from the same cloth, you probably would have wanted to financially support your son when it mattered - because all the pickleball playing frat bros names Brayden at the country club with picturesque wedding photos , literally all of them, graduated with a 2.1 GPA in business and got their jobs through nepotism". there are no frat bros in boater shoes playing ultimate frisbee whos dads refuse to support them. it doesnt exist.

now, i am not entitled to financial support. just like my dad isnt entitled to grandkids. but these two things are related, even if you want to throw a tantrum and be vulgar. it changes nothing

you seem to be confused and upset. try to detach a bit from your heightened emotional state

62442391
>>62442389
Look at his other post it’s obvious he’s mentally ill and just lashing out at random people on fucking 4 Chan of all places

62442419
>>62442346
Let's take it easy and look first into the mirror before judging others. Not everyone is fit to be a parent. Hopefully your child inherits a good stepfather, which would be a better outcome than inheriting your will.

62442422
>>62442391
what i am trying to articulate in my post might require an IQ of 110 to maybe process. because its slightly nuanced. but its really not that complicated, fundamentally.

my dad wanted a WASP-y son who had fratbro hobbies and a kind of upper class lifestyle. golf, wedding photos, friend group of likeminded frat bros who go to sports games and go out drinking together. but my dad was convinced by talking heads like buffet and ramsey that any financial support for your kids after their early 20s is wrong. so he took that advice and cut off all support. and i, having no support in college, basically had to learn totally by myself how to function and rely on just myself - to the point of becoming self reliant, at the cost of having no appetite for frivolous spending, luxury, or expensive/high maintenance relationships once i entered the workforce. understand- i am not complaining whatsoever, i am just saying, if you want a preppy rich kid socialite son with a hot high maintenance girlfriend, youre probably gonna need to get him a job. if you dont want to because that will spoil him or whatever, thats perfectly fine, just dont be surprised when he finds his own financial advice (eg sam hyde lol), like you found yours, and he decides to go full hermitmaxx mode and he daytrades with his paychecks instead of buying golf clubs or a saving up for an annual trip to europe for his high maintenance hot gf

does this make any sense? am i crazy here?

62442430
>>62442422

son you need to go MAKE SOMETHING of yourself

62442437
>>62442422
Your father is wrong to dictate the life he wants you to live. You are meant to bond with your parents if you both have a good relationship, sometimes helping each other if possible and within reason. Never burden a child like that.

62442543
>>62438432
don't you see the "it is in a different currency"?
it's obviously not in euros u dumbass. European countries have a few more currencies.

62442989
>>62442194
>>62442389
>>62442422
cringe and victimcomplex

62443265
>>62442989
Dads who want to control the lives of their kids without supporting them are just delusional retards as evidenced by the lashing out in response to those posts.

If my boss wants me to do work for him, he pays me. If a business wants me to try their product, they give me incentives for a trial period. If daddy wants his son to live a certain way it's going to take more than a regal command. You wanted to teach your kid self reliance by kicking him out, right? Well now your kid doesn't rely on you and has zero obligation to follow your desired path. Mission accomplished, you successfully removed yourself from having influence.

62443320
>>62442989
>changing his ID
>still trying to lash out at random posters

Yeah the only sad lonely faggot here is you

62443525
26% of US men 25-29 live at home. If you are single and have a good relationship with your family there really is no reason not to do it, you can get some decent money together

item.title: I'm broke as fuck, right now trying to learn some FOREX shit but it takes too much time to learn all the necessary shit… — 15 replies — 46.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm broke as fuck, right now trying to learn some FOREX shit but it takes too much time to learn all the necessary shit…

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-03 19:49 UTC

OP

I'm broke as fuck, right now trying to learn some FOREX shit but it takes too much time to learn all the necessary shit so what else can I try to stop being broke ass? What made y'all 6 figures or more?

Early replies (first 3)

62432407
Going to my job for a year and some change

62432477
Train up and join the military at the beginning of the next fiscal year. Join a trade like elevator mechanic. Do something with ai-arbitage, idk man.

62432500
>>62432477
Let me guess. Loser dad?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62432524
>>62432371
Being a better person made me a six figure income in fiscal year 2025. God helps you pay your bills if you are making a sustained effort at being a better person. Start by making a list of your worst qualities and behaviours.

62436100
>>62432371
What's the problemerino, forex-seeking anon?

62438490
>>62432371
son, you need to learn about the twin miracles:
Grit & Compounding

Grit: Slaving away as a wagie and when you get off work either going to a second job, or learning some skills and keeping at it until you excape the hand-to-mouth syndrome. Only ~25% of people are able to do this in capitalist societies; more like 4% in non-capitalist societies.

Compounding: Would you rather have a million dollars today or 1 penny today compounded at 100% daily for a year? take the latter because you would have more than $5 million at the end of the month.

Point being - work hard, save what little you can, and invest it. It will take time, but you will see it accumulate in time. Remember the "rule of 72" - basically a 10% gain compounded yearly doubles your money in 7.2 years.

So - say you save $2,000 a year. in five years you will have $14,652
In 10 years, that's $40,250
In 20: $140,000

And then the other two best pieces of financial advice I know are: Don't get divorced & buy a duplex

62438493
>>62438490
also:

How old are you and where?

62438942
dayum it's over 10% in past 10 years?
I worked for 3 years out of past 11 years and have most of it stashed away in a lazy target date fund.
Had I kept working for 10 I could've paid in full a whole-ass house and car in the midwest. Instead the plan is my autist ass gets our house when my parents croak because everyone else are well off and don't even want our house.

62439003
>>62438490
27 , Tucson

62439253
>>62432371
>FX
don't do it

62440990
Test

62442283
>tuc town, my city be pimpin

62442315
>>62432371
Broke people have no use for ROI, since they have no I. Just fucking work man.

62443474
>>62442283
I'm positive this site is region locked and other anons are browsing an entirely different catalog

62443602
>>62432371
You cant forex if you don't have money.

item.title: >YOU'RE A MILLION DOLLARS IN DEBT? NOOOOOOOOO YOU CAN'T DECLARE BANKRUPTCY LIKE I DID. YOU BETTER WORK 3 FULL TIME JOBS… — 12 replies — 25.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>YOU'RE A MILLION DOLLARS IN DEBT? NOOOOOOOOO YOU CAN'T DECLARE BANKRUPTCY LIKE I DID. YOU BETTER WORK 3 FULL TIME JOBS…

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-07-03 19:40 UTC

OP

>YOU'RE A MILLION DOLLARS IN DEBT? NOOOOOOOOO YOU CAN'T DECLARE BANKRUPTCY LIKE I DID. YOU BETTER WORK 3 FULL TIME JOBS WITH SIDE HUSTLES AND SHIT RICE AND BEANS UNTIL EVERY LAST CENT IS PAID IN FULL

Why are boomers such hypocrites?

Early replies (first 3)

62441296
>>62441262

> I don’t want you to make the same mistakes I did, son

62442584
>>62441262
>I refuse to learn from your misteaks just let me fuck my life up in pieces

62442587
>>62441296
>>62442584
Why would anyone take financial advice from someone who went bankrupt

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442596
>>62442587
being able to put yourself back together after a calamity is a skill some people could learn from
ramsay is a grifter though

62442597
>>62442587
a) They can recognize the signs of somebody making their mistake, and give valid advice on how to avoid it/recover from it.
b) He who has never failed has never tried.
c) The perfect is the enemy of the good.
d) Your mother will die in her sleep tonight.

62442744
>>62442596
>>62442597
Fuck off Dave

62442749
>>62442587
Why would I take financial advice from someone who was always rich? They don't understand how hard it is for someone like me....

Can't win

62442805
>>62442597
Fuck you

62442881
>>62442597

62443344
>>62442749
>They don't understand how hard it is for someone like me....
This attitude is why you'll always be poor. You enjoy being poor, it gives you something to whine about, and in your feeble mind it can be used as an excuse for your continual failures. I used to be poor but my attitude was always "what can I do to become rich and how do I execute on that" not "oh woe is me I'm so poor why won't people feel sorry for me".

62443456
>>62443344
The point is you'd be a better person to learn financial planning from than someone who was born rich and just coasted.

62443568
>>62442749
If I wanted diet advice id rather get it from someone who used to be fat than someone who was always fit.

item.title: THE LARGEST BULL RUN IN HISTORY HAS BEGUN — 15 replies — 13.5/hr

62442316

you fucking retard, kek

62442323
>>62442297
is it finally our time, breths?

62442328
>>62442297
no

62442512
ey yo, where my efferiumz bro's at?! we wrappin and stackin yet!?

62442549
>>62442512
we here cuh we finna make fat stacks off dis shi fr

62442551
>>62442297
Believe in somETHing

0x7c9f94a79b6b859dfb1d3312fc2311b39f89c677

62442667
Let's fucking go!

62442669
>>62442667
Kek that pic

62442732
>>62442667
You bastard. I click to goon but is vitalik

62442925
>>62442732
Still goon Vitamin vagene, Sar.

62443454
>>62442297
>largest bullrun ever
>down 5%
why are crypto investors like this?

62443461

62434662

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 199 replies — 67.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 199 • Updated: 2026-07-03 17:11 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423064
Good to see the thread is still going strong. Today is my off day, let's look into the next subject "predictive programming"

We'll start with Oliver tree

62425537
op was not a fag this thread has good vibes we should keep it going until we all make it
personally im trading perps with a system after 10 years of learning charts off/on but finally committing
did a 32x in 2021 and should have been able to retire but didnt anticipate the biden years so here we are going pure psycho mode like we did back then but even harder until money is /solved/, then I'll keep trading for the love of the game cuz it's the best sport in the world

62425849
>>62423064
This shit is way too schizo. No anon you can not predict the future. You can maybe foresee some type of market trends, seasonality or whatever but this is just ridiculous

62425890
>>62425537
Got any good tips/ressources on learning to trade charts? trying to get into it too

62427228
>>62425849
I concede. I'll look for a more productive venture but I won't let the thread or general die, it will be some time before value is struck, I will admit that. I can be tenacious if nothing else.

62428130
>>62425890
emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion

62428170
>pic related
>"...focus on the chart as these information is very likely to be priced in already"
the "TA is astrology for men" line is used to make fun of retards who take it too far and treat it like divination when in reality it's all stacking probabilities/confluence in your favor as best you can, and from the above example you can see relying on your feed for information will almost always be slower than the chart

62428488
Finally some good posts

62428503
>>62428141
Big trout doesn't post on Twitter and his TG hasn't had activity since May. Any other suggestions?

62428780
>>62428503
His tg is BigTroutTrade and he posts on twitter all the time wdym

62428797
>>62428780
BigTrout Trading on youtube as well

other X goats that have been killing it IMO recently:
@MoneyPrinter0x
and my personal favorite currently:
@Wild_Randomness

there's tons of alpha just follow those people, then see what they repost, then follow the people they interact with that provide research, and even or especially follow the chinese/korean/japanese accounts that post comprehensive semiconductor/other market effort posts since translation is seamless now
never been a better time to go full NEET

62429207
>>62428797
But you're still under $10k?

62429246
>>62378965
Anon Belgium is literally doing very well in the world cup, you should make more of these predictions

62431586
bump.

62431599
probably nothing
bio protocol desci ai BIO now cookin with open ai, nvidia, anthropic, claude news check twita

62432055
>>62373752
Hey
I just started out in this crypto thing and bought $450 worth of solana. How do I become cool and multiply it

62432088
>>62432055
Be like me and listen to people within a group of other retards that are pushing a token. The last one tanked a $200 investment -98% but then he re-deployed the same token and it yielded a 40x from where it was called.

62432306
>>62432088
pardon my incompetence but I didn't understand much of what you just wrote anon

62432350
>>62428797
I had bad experiences following twitter people and waiting for signals even those that seem smart. It can be interesting but you’ll be lost if they randomly stop posting, they’re not funds giving you alpha but people that should have better things to do than post if their full job is actually trading.

I do still like @donalt and @cryptocred their show technical roundup is a rare non clickbaity show that just focuses on TA but most people are inherently frauds on social media

62432360
>>62432055
I don’t think Solana is a good buy right now it’s barely holding support and likely going lower to like 40 if I remember correctly

>>62432088
If you have a group like that be a hero to the community and share it to us

62432373
>>62432360
>be a hero to the community and share it to us
see
>>62385756
I recently held an airdrop for /biz/ too

https://warosu.org/biz/thread/62424715

62434369
Bump, I'm getting an early weekend because of the country's birthday. Will devote time to this thread.

62435112
>>62432350
skill issue. donalt? lol
you gotta follow the small accounts anon

62436077
>>62435112
Why would you rely on random accounts calls and not build the skill yourself? You are just fucked if you only listen to others, and you have no way to know they aren’t just lucky for a while until they drag you down with themselves

>donalt
Yes he may be old and washed but you can still learn a lot from two traders that never shilled some memecoin. Except for ftx but I guess everyone had an ftx sponsorship for a while

62436265
>UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD
>200 REPLIES

LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOO OH NO NO NO NO AAAAAAAHHHHHH

62437580
>>62436077
did you read my first post? i literally said not to just blindly copy trade
there's nothing wrong with using great traders as confluence

62440778
bamp.

62442413
Bump

62443230
Man i wanna move to SLC someday

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 52 replies — 65.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-07-03 19:21 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62441565
>Fraud payout.
>700 per bobby.
>$30916500 deposited in my Fidelity account
>40% aside for taxes
>60% dumped ito Teddy/GME
>MOASS
>????
>I have a threesome with platnum sparkles and Amelie Warren

62441579
Holy shit I just got $500 million deposited to my account!

62441599
>>62441565
so when you take off platnum's pants and her cock is bigger than yours is that a good thing or a bad thing

62441611
HOLY FUCK HOOOOLY FUCK HOLY SHIT FUCK
BATHROOM KINGS
BATHROOM GODS
WE DID IT WE WON
CELEBRATE FUCKING HARD FRIENDS

62441798
>>62441320
Yup. We gonna be kangs.
>and billionares<

62441800
>>62441565
Pretty sure fraud payouts are NOT taxed. But someone can correct me.

62441804
>>62441800
Ive checked a few times and arrived at the conclusion they are taxed. We be amazing to roll everything into gme though

62441869
>>62441800
When you get a fraud payout the IRS pays you 50%

62441988
>>62441800
it wont be officially labelled as fraud settlement.

62442185
>>62441544

62442189
>>62442185
Isn't that a BILLIONAIRE? Why is he talking about the CUSIP Conundrum?

62442681
>>62442189
>he’s on the list for the $GABBBYQ GAPE

62442725
GET FUCKING READY BECAUSE WERE GOING TO MAKE IT WERE ALL GOING TO MAKE IT!!!

62442960
>>62442725
>>62441611
>>62441544
>>62441346

I hate you SO fucking much, Alex. I need you to intimately know this fact so that when you're sitting in that citric acid bath in that Baltimore warehouse, slowly cooking through your degloved dick, hands, and feet, watching me fuck your wife, you'll know why it's happening to you and that no screaming, begging or haggling will save you from the rusted cauldron you will ultimately die in.

Yea yea...muh "why do they hate alex", "youre not him", etc. etc..
Fuck you nigger.

62442967
But yea....
WAGMI bobbies!!!!!

62442975
not obsessed btw

62442981
>>62442975
Everywhere they look they see him.

62442986
>im not him
lol
nigger(s)

62442997
>>62442960
Total Towel Triumph

62443002
>>62442960
PPseeds is important to the bbbyq community. Weird that you hate him so much, especially since he is friends with Bill Pulte.

62443011
>>62443002
>PPseeds is important to the bbbyq community. Weird that you hate him so much, especially since he is friends with Bill Pulte.
>cmon bro dont cook me in a cauldron in baltimore
>doing the EXACT thing I said you would do

LMFAO !!!
M
F
You mean nothing to the community nigger !
A
O
WAGMI !!!

62443025
shills in shambles

62443041
BBBYQ CONGRATULATIONS CLASS 9

62443078
Queen PP will be holding a gathering this weekend at the holiday inn, check PP x account for invite lists

62443090
>>62443078
It will be quite the "merger" if you know what I mean

62443203
>>62443078

62443244
WAGMI

62443367
Why such satirical vitriol against the host of a podcast that was frequented by the director of national intelligence? Even Larry Cheng went on once. Looks like it was a way for LC and Pulte to signal under a cover of plausible deniability. Wallstreet seethed so hard they had main Reddit admins shutdown /r/theppshow. Doug cifu spilled his spaghetti so hard he was force retired and banned from hockey involvement because he seethed so hard about an obscure internet bbbyq recovery theory community. Then accounting for RC’s ties with bbbyq, there is a stampede of elephants in the room at this point that bot swarms and paid stock bashers insist are all irrelevant and offtopic or nothingburgers if you try to mention them.

Interesting times.

62443473
not reading any of that
fuck anyone that is not rc or rk especially hooked nosed jewtubers

WAGMI !!!!

62443510
>>62443367
I trust PP's DD

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 94 replies — 50.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-07-03 18:16 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62438081
>>62433854
Time will tell which of us got it right.

62438118
>>62426537
cool, sounds like mumus will have a "great" time.

62438272
>>62438118
What's that?

62438282
>>62426512
I soldeded months ago. I dont believe in btc and never had. BTC couldnt even outperform motherfucking spy for the past 5 years so dont give me """""zoom out""""". more money to be made in stocks anway, no reason to be married to bitcoin.

62438284
>>62426512
>What's your strategy?
Apeing every goycheck.

62438322
>>62437565
ngmi

62438328
>>62438284
Buy kendu and ansem

62438410
>>62438328
No.

62439167
>>62433595
Saw the Aegis partnership, now Utila too. They're lining up some solid collaborations.

62439181
>>62437631
loved the confidence

62439236
>>62438282
then why are you here

62439758
>>62438074
With that risk model, I don't think anyone needs to hand over their custody.

Self custody is part of what it's designed for.

62440739
>>62439758
what about in an exchange like binance

62441904
>>62439167
Maybe they have more coming that we just don't know about yet. It's actually a good sign that they're still building despite the current market conditions.

62442213
>>62440739
Kraken

62442214
>>62426512
Buy the dip, obviously.

62442227
>>62439167
But that won't help the price up

62442228
>>62442214
Now???

62442255
>>62426512
I dca 60% of my savings each month while saving the remaining 40% for potential lower lows as we approach October, at which point I'll start going in with all of my monthly savings and that 40% I've been holding back.

62442503
>>62442227
Maybe not immediately. But without infrastructure, there's nothing to drive long-term value anyway.

62442507
>>62442214
Yup, little by little.

62442511
>>62442507
>>62442228
I dollar cost. THe past few months have been gr8

62442553
>>62442511
Where do you do that

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

62442650
>>62442503
memecoins pump faster. I made money on Ansem and some Solana gems

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

item.title: VOO and Chill — 181 replies — 40.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 181 • Updated: 2026-07-03 15:42 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427417
I am also curious about DRAM can anyone tell me if it’s a good hold for a short while

62428031
jfc DRAM (ie a thematic ETF) is bullshit made for retard retail investors that get their financial advice from grifters off of tiktok. this happens every time a finance topic hits the news and every time people get burned.
barely worth saying. if you're unironically taking advice from /biz/ you're already doomed intellectually.

62428054
>>62428031
but why exactly, what reason is there to believe the companies within it won't continue to turn a profit for the foreseeable future?

62428080
>>62428031
>>62428054
Like I'm serious, what exactly is wrong about holding it and selling once memory cools down, is it smarter to just go into a more volatile ETF that at least has a longer history if you want more returns, is it just hype and not really the next big thing, explain why

62428238
>>62428080
this is basic finance 101, there are plenty of resources online or off that will explain it to you.
ignorance isn't even the issue here, your reaction to not knowing something is to have random retards on a thinly veiled gambling board tell you what to think. and don't just jump on another grifter selling a course or ask some AI slop engine and ask it leading questions like "why is investing in DRAM a good idea?".

62428337
>>62428238
In other words you don't know

62429683
>>62400493
Anon there is nothing wrong with doing VOO and chill if you have time and principal
Going into wildly volatile ETFs can be okay if it’s a satellite account
Just be careful of FOMO and not being risk averse
You sound like you have a good head on your shoulders though
Remember this is money, real money, your money

62430693
VOO anons where you at?

62430902
>>62400493
Contrarian nerds always lose.
Always.
In middle school dating.
Being high school incels.
In college.
At work.
In love.
At investing.
At all times.
Follow the crowd and try to fit in. Everyone doing anything else is a reject loser.

62431131
>>62430902
Who is being the contrarian here though

62431374
VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses, Michelle Louie & Aurélie Denis. So with your 0.03% management fee you're essentially paying for those do-nothing "day in the life of a portfolio manager" videos for daughters of elite sociopaths.

62431402
>>62431374
A small price to pay for a low expense ratio

62431920
>>62431131
Anyone trying to beat VOO over decades.

62431922
>>62431920
Feel like VUG has consistently beaten VOO though

62431971
>>62431922
Contrarian.
I own VUG and don't own VOO tho :)

62432023
>>62431920
fair enough
thoughts on a younger person doing QQQM?

62434054
Anyone have thoughts or realized returns and experiences on VOO + satellite
accounts instead of just VOO

62435372
Was gonna try to go into this DRAM dip but decided to just do more VOO
But idk a higher return ETF still sounds promising and like a no brainer whether it’s SOXX or QQQM but QQQ has so much crossover with VOO it’s just redundant and only good for growth seasons
I guess this is why VOO and Chill exists to just stop thinking

62436303
>>62431920
What about QQQ though

62436357
i boughted my daily VOO
i'll keep dealing the real world like a good goyim in the mean time.

62436478
>>62436357
huzzah brother

62436499
>>62400493
Where my VGT bros at?

62436507
>>62400636
>Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....
You say this as if the average /biz/ shitcoin gambler is performing better than VOO which is definitely not the case.

62436510
>>62428031
Your IQ is so actually low. Holy shit.

62436538
>>62436510
Do you think DRAM has value, honest question, and for how long - if so, what dip should one wait for
>>62436499
VGT and QQQ are only doing better thanks to tech having such strong performances for years now, correct?
VOO seems safe since you don't know the future, but on the other hand, thinking the future of the market for the next decades wont always be tech focused seems so short sighted it's silly.....maybe I should start DCA into VGT?

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

62438775
Yep bros
Bought some more VOO and am waiting on my DRAM position to go green so I can sell and not have to deal with thematic ETFs no high expense ratios ever again
Simple as

62440169
>>62431374
>VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses
VOO is passively managed. These 2 girls are not like Kathy Wood picking stocks.

62442752
>>62431374
They could boil alive eat grannies alive for all I care as long as they get the money going, what kind of faggot ass train of thought is this. They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding. "The people making me rich are foids so it makes me icky" is unironically the foidest take I have ever read.
>>62430902
A year ago I started investing for the first time in my life (I'm in my mid 20s) and thought I was very smart and I would just outperform the market 1% profit at a time. After a whole year I only managed to get a net benefit of a 3% after wasting many hours and carrying heavy bags. I learnt my lesson. VOO and chill.

62442988
>>62442752
>They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding.

The portfolio managers of VOO have 0 control over whether VOO compounds or not. VOO is simply an ETF that tracks the S&P 500. Their only job is to make sure VOO follows the S&P 500. Once the fund is set up, there really is not much for them to do. I guess if they haven't automated removing and adding companies to the composition of VOO, then they do that.

item.title: sony is stopping disc releases for PS games you know what that means — 45 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
sony is stopping disc releases for PS games you know what that means

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-07-03 18:16 UTC

OP

sony is stopping disc releases for PS games
you know what that means

Early replies (first 3)

62434070
Believe it or not -- calls.
(/v/ermin might disagree)

62434082
>>62434061
So they're using GTA as the 800 lb gorilla to strong arm this through.

There's literally no reason not to abandon consoles completely and go full PC at this point. And I was a diehard Sonybro a few years ago. Then one day a lightbulb clicked, no new gens to buy, no backwards compatibility issues, I can play all my games in one place, with superior graphics, forever.

Yeah fuck this noise.

62434586
>>62434061
Every company:
>how can we gradually make our products worse?
Why though?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62434884
>>62434061
Lmao who the fuck still buys vidya in 2026. You have piracy. There's about 10,000 solid vidya to play over the past 40 years. Download an emulator and get on it faggots

62434892
>>62434082
get all your roms and emulators and back it up and keep one offline emulator PC/laptop for the future.

62434906
>>62434737
>just baghold the shitty $50 game for 10+ years to have that 1 greasy mystery meat mf trying to negrotiate it down to treedfiddy bro

62435107
>>62434731
I threw away like 2 grand of ps3 game discs because I just don't care

62435164
>>62434864
That's not capitalism tho, that's gov't regulation

62435169
Online games are easier to monetize. It's simple economics. Look how much money GTA online has made in the past decade selling DLC to kids.

62435290
>>62435107
rip

62435322
>>62435164
No anon. Government regulations are a good thing and help the consumer. Do you not remember when Apple was forced to drop their proprietary charging cables in favor of USB-C? That was government regulations that did that. Or don't tell me that you'd rather be forced to get new proprietary charging cables with every new phone you got?

62435373
>>62435322
Government regulation is literally what forces companies to disregard investing in their employees and the long term in favor of chasing higher quarterly profits for their investors, which is what that post was a complaint about.

62435392
>>62435373
Goverments USED to do that when they actually had taxes in the 70s and earlier, all the extra declared profits would just get taxed so the companies were forced to either spend it on themselves or give it away, and they spent it on themselves through reinvestment and employees.
Then Reagan happened and "trickle down economics" was sold to the masses that if we just cut regulations, businesses will still spend on themselves instead of chasing profits. Cutting regulations led to heaps of shitty bad loans being spread out through the mortgage market causing the 08 collapse lol.
You're a clown.
Lol.

62435404
>>62434061
> trends in consumer preference

62435437
>>62434061
Consoles and gaymes on disc media is ancient games slop, nigga for goodness sake Stream released on pc like 25 years ago
I haven't used physical media installs since the fucking early 2000s

games don't fit, updates don't fit, nothing fits

physical media is pure shit for games

early consoles with cartridges and when games fit on a single cdrom was a different era

>mah I wont own anything!
fuck off physical copies if it's a real game, if it's live content games that can be pulled because it's on servers I don't care it's slop anyway, just play it enjoy the experience then realize life is futile and nothnigs lasts forever

62435625
>>62434843
No dickhead, but if there's no disc anyway why the fuck not just switch to PC?

62435679
>>62435437
Ok schlomo

62436053
>>62434061
Sony:
>invent multiple iterations of laser disc technology
Also Sony:
>abandon all forms of laser disc technology

62436792
>>62434061
Bro's if you bought a ton of xenogears disks, you would be rich. Only a 25 year old!

62436793
>>62434082
Damn Rockstar and Sony working behind the scenes?

62436884
>>62434061
There goes the one thing consoles had over PC.
Probably also an indicator that none of the games coming out after 2027 will be worth playing.

62436948
>>62434061
The console itself is next, PS6 and Xbox Helix will be the last physical consoles, the next gen will just be cloud gaming. This also kills piracy, as no one will ever have physical access to the game data. They may have small console boxes to handle the streaming, but all the processing power will be on the servers.

62436955
>>62435107
the fact you didnt take them for recycling or just give them to some kid on the street is why it’s good the goyim will
own nothing

62437062
>>62434864
>let's not improve the product to increase profits
>let's hire more transqueens and jiggies who are too stupid to understand that we will axe them as soon as their pay increases, just let them cut the corners 1st
I beg to differ.

62440660
>>62436793
Not necessarily. But if the biggest game of the next 2 generations is going discless now we have a pretext.

62442716
>>62435322
>fiscal policy, corporate regulation, and case law all drive a fundamental endless growth business mentality via incentive structure and limited liability.
>but i got cheap phone cables tho
your opinion of the world is informed in the same way as that of a small child

62442746
I have around 30 ps3 discs (near mint, in box with booklet), what am I in for

62442815
>>62434061
Last physical game I ever bought was World of Warcraft, burning crusade
Get with the times grandpa
If you want to keep the game, just pirate it

62442831
>>62434061
Gamers are such retards kek. Wallets getting raped by infaltion and cost of living and having Epstein presidents. And goyim care about discs that don’t even have the full game on it anyways lmao

62442843
>>62434061
disc's are already obsolete , whats the point of buying physical when you still need to download the game and its add-ons via internet and still can't play it without an internet access even if its singleplayer.

62442854
>>62434620
>Does it mean that SSDs are going to be more expensive
If people used Gaystation still it would.

62443382
>>62434082
I fucking hate the file sizes of new games.
Id they want high res games like reddead2 or gtaV or far crys 5/6 to be played.... I am going to need a disk I can play without the need for internet.

62443385
>>62434620
Supposedly, 1tb dvd discs become available this year.

item.title: How Much Did You GAIN in Crypto Last Year? — 21 replies — 19.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
How Much Did You GAIN in Crypto Last Year?

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-07-03 19:03 UTC

OP

> President Donald Trump reported nearly $1.2 billion in earnings last year, with the bulk of the income coming from cryptocurrency ventures and branded merchandise, according to a new federal financial disclosure.

>The filing shows more than $500 million from World Liberty Financial and over $600 million from meme coin sales through CIC Digital. Trump also earned millions more from sales of branded Bibles, sneakers, and watches.

>The disclosure highlights how Trump’s newer business ventures are generating more revenue than parts of his traditional real estate empire.

>Are you surprised by how much Trump made outside of real estate?

Early replies (first 3)

62433082
>>62433062
no

62433083
>>62433062
This 1 Post Is Good. Yaaaaayyy 2026!

62433085
>>62433062
I lost 1.2B last year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62435388
>>62433062
UPDATE! It was just blind luck!

62435393
>>62435388
how come politicians are so lucky?

62435784
>>62435393
They’re smart!

62438018
>>62433062
i lost 1.2 billion on Trump Melania and Liberty FInance
I literally got rugged in all 3 of them, by hte kike president of the US
He and all off his useless family should get actually lynced for that shit
why are we sitting here, after we lose all of our money to this faggot and than we congratulate him on the money he stole from us?
Why are we so fcking retarded?

62438031
Seems like the big money is in selling crypto and not buying it.

62438178
>>62433062
>fleeced by the Trumps
Kek it couldn't have happened to more deserving people.

62438256
>>62438018
>why are we sitting here, after we lose all of our money to this faggot
>we
Why are you sitting here after YOU lost money to him?

>why are we so retarded?
>we
You are. I didn’t lose money to him.

62438288
>>62433062
Still better than socialism.

62441494
>>62438256
Should he be standing?

62441857
>>62438018
should we be standing?

62442030
I hope America gets glassed July 4th.

62442034
>>62441857
why not, whats wrong with standing
standing up against the jewish takeover

62443290
>>62442034
>im willing to stand up to the jews!
>b..but you guys go first heh..
lol

62443295
I personally lost 2.4 billion on crypto. Where did the rest of it go?

62443401
>>62433062

I would be mad that the sitting president of the US is rug pulling his citizen like a like a 17 year old tik toker, but then again it only affects miggers so it's funny.

62443440
I wonder if this narrative will kill Clarity Act till way later

62443444
>>62443401
These are the "people" who got "scammed". Bunch of degenerate gamblers.

62443462
0. (I hold link)

62442771

62443188
>>62442771

i need copium

62443215

>>62443188
Why would this time be any different?

62443228
>>62442769

62443327
>>62442764
we shouldn't have listened to that chink.

62443363
>>62442764
We literally can't stop winning, it's not fair, not fair at all fellow SPCX chads

62443369
>>62442764
>0.00 24h returns

62443466
>>62442764
Don't worry, bobro, the market is VERY long. They are going to punish bulls.

62443506
>>62442764
>this weak ass euphoria pump
It will fail like all the others.

62443529
Are you all genuinely retarded? It's a three day trading weekend. When all markets are otherwise closed there is lower volume and the bots can wash trade the price up. Then Saylor can dump you next week.

item.title: Five years since I bought 1 ICP for £154 — 21 replies — 14.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Five years since I bought 1 ICP for £154

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-07-03 18:40 UTC

OP

Five years since I bought 1 ICP for £154

Early replies (first 3)

62438170
>>62438105
yikes

62438389
>>62438105
Meh that aint a big loss.
There are cunts that loss 6 figures to that shit

62438426
i dont give a fuck faggot LOL

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62438461
>>62438105
Haha, people did this

62438471
>>62438105
congrats on your x0.0100

62438539
I'm starting to have doubts about moonman

62438624
>>62438105
what would happen (hypothetically of course) if i was to fart in that nozzle whilst she slept?

62438638
>>62438105
I'm having terrible sleep for the past few years and I want to get a sleep study but it cost 1500+ to do it

62440342
>>62438638
samesies

62440432
>>62438638
The best I;ve slept in my life was doing like 17-21 hours on then a 9 hour sleep period. As you can imagine this does no work for modern societal living but damn did I ever sleep fantastic and wake up feeling amazing.

62440599
>>62438105
That's you listening to Insane Clowne Posse on your Walkmen in the photo?

62440675
>>62438638
Just buy a whoop and sleep with it for a couple of weeks to get a decent average. Almost all pro athletes use it for in-depth sleep analysis

62440744
>>62440675
thafacksawhoop?

62441605
>>62438638
get insurance, the study is super cheap if you have the right insurance, and then make sure to look for any stipulation that you have to keep the insurance plan or use the machine a specific amount of time. If you are clean and maintain it the replacable parts are pretty cheap and the machine itself will run forever

62441786
>>62438105
I remember when it was getting shilled here after its listing on coinbase
It was $600 and shills were INSISTING that it was worth way more

62441790
>>62438638
Try this:
>no eating for at least 3 hours before bed
>no alcohol
>limit caffeine intake, and don't have any after noon.
>no screens for at least 30 minutes before bed
>keep lighting low at night
>keep room temp below 70
>use black out curtains
>sleep on your side, or elevate your bed if you absolutely must sleep on your back
>use a nasal dilator or nose strips to increase airflow
>hum for about a minute before bed; it increase nitric oxide and relaxes airways
>change your sheets regularly, make sure you don't have mold in your room
>replace your pillow if it's more than 2 years old
>be consistent about all of this for 3 weeks

If you're not doing better after 3 weeks of this see a doctor.

62441818
>>62438105
>1
This is barely loss. I lost considerably more on mutant salmon.

62441830
>>62438105
ICP is trading at $2.02, only 9.05% above it's all time low. Things couldn't get any worse, right?? LMAO

62443381
>>62438105
I've sunken an average of about $30K into ICP over the past few years and my stack is now worth about $10K.

62443409
>>62438638
what does a sleep study do? like i feel like my sleep is okay but ive obv got no comparison

62443424
Help me Internet Computer!
